categories.name: Internal Approval & Governance
categories.description: Secure board approval and establish governance framework for the Bitcoin treasury strategy.

items.title: Commission independent analysis of Bitcoin treasury strategy
items.description: Engage an independent adviser to prepare a comprehensive analysis of the risks, opportunities, regulatory implications, and financial impact of a Bitcoin treasury allocation.

items.title: Prepare and present board paper
items.description: Draft a detailed board paper covering investment rationale, proposed allocation, custody arrangements, risk assessment, disclosure obligations, and governance framework. Present at a board meeting.

items.title: Obtain formal board resolution
items.description: Pass a board resolution approving the Bitcoin treasury policy. Ensure detailed minutes record the discussion, considerations, professional advice relied upon, and any conflicts of interest declared.

items.title: Establish delegated authority framework
items.description: Define who can authorise Bitcoin acquisitions and disposals, and at what thresholds board approval is required versus management delegation.

categories.name: ASX/ASIC Disclosure Requirements
categories.description: Comply with continuous disclosure obligations and prepare market communications.

items.title: Obtain legal advice on ASX disclosure obligations
items.description: Engage corporate lawyers to advise on continuous disclosure requirements under LR 3.1, whether shareholder approval is needed under LR 11.1, and any ASIC regulatory considerations.

items.title: Prepare ASX announcement
items.description: Draft a market announcement disclosing the Bitcoin treasury strategy. Include: rationale, allocation parameters, custody arrangements, risk management approach, and governance framework.

items.title: Brief major shareholders and broker
items.description: Before the public announcement, brief the company's broker and prepare talking points for engagement with major institutional shareholders.

items.title: Establish ongoing disclosure protocol
items.description: Create an internal protocol for when Bitcoin-related information triggers continuous disclosure obligations (material acquisitions, significant losses, security incidents).

categories.name: Legal & Compliance Setup
categories.description: Ensure legal and regulatory compliance for the Bitcoin treasury program.

items.title: Engage corporate lawyer with digital asset experience
items.description: Appoint or consult with a corporate lawyer experienced in digital assets, ASX listing rules, and Corporations Act directors' duties.

items.title: Review director conflict of interest obligations
items.description: Ensure all directors disclose any material personal interest in cryptocurrency under s 191 Corporations Act. Update the conflict of interest register.

items.title: Draft Bitcoin treasury policy
items.description: Prepare a formal Bitcoin Treasury Policy document covering allocation limits, risk parameters, custody requirements, disclosure triggers, and review schedule.

items.title: Update risk management framework
items.description: Integrate Bitcoin-specific risks (volatility, custody, regulatory, reputational) into the company's existing risk register and risk management framework.

categories.name: Accounting & Tax Preparation
categories.description: Set up accounting processes and tax compliance for Bitcoin holdings.

items.title: Brief external auditor
items.description: Discuss the Bitcoin treasury strategy with the external auditor. Agree on audit procedures for verifying existence, ownership, and fair value of Bitcoin holdings.

items.title: Determine accounting treatment
items.description: Confirm the accounting standard to apply (AASB 138 intangible asset or alternative), measurement model (cost vs revaluation), and disclosure requirements. Document the accounting policy election.

items.title: Engage tax adviser for corporate crypto tax strategy
items.description: Consult with a tax adviser on CGT vs trading stock classification, franking account implications, and transfer pricing considerations for any multinational group entities.

items.title: Set up financial reporting processes
items.description: Establish processes for fair value measurement at each reporting date, unrealised gain/loss calculations, note disclosures, and management commentary on Bitcoin holdings.

categories.name: Custody & Security Setup
categories.description: Establish institutional-grade custody for Bitcoin holdings.

items.title: Select an institutional custody provider
items.description: Evaluate and select a regulated institutional custodian with SOC 2 Type II certification, segregated accounts, insurance coverage, and independent verification capabilities.

items.title: Execute custody agreement
items.description: Negotiate and execute a custody agreement covering security standards, insurance, liability, reporting, audit access, and termination provisions.

items.title: Establish internal security protocols
items.description: Set up multi-party authorisation for withdrawals, access controls, monitoring, and incident response procedures.

items.title: Verify insurance coverage
items.description: Confirm the custodian's insurance covers digital asset loss. Consider whether the company's own D&O and professional indemnity insurance needs updating.

categories.name: Banking & Fiat On-ramp
categories.description: Set up banking and exchange infrastructure for Bitcoin acquisitions.

items.title: Confirm bank supports exchange transfers
items.description: Verify with the company's banking partner that transfers to cryptocurrency exchanges are permitted. Brief the relationship manager on the Bitcoin treasury strategy.

items.title: Open accounts with AUSTRAC-registered exchanges
items.description: Register corporate accounts on regulated Australian exchanges with deep AUD/BTC liquidity. Complete corporate KYC verification.

items.title: Evaluate OTC desk options
items.description: For large transactions, evaluate OTC desks that can execute block trades with minimal market impact. Establish trading agreements and credit lines.

categories.name: First Purchase Execution
categories.description: Execute the initial Bitcoin acquisition with proper controls and disclosure.

items.title: Execute test transaction
items.description: Conduct a small test purchase to verify the complete workflow: bank transfer, exchange purchase, withdrawal to custody, accounting entry, and audit trail.

items.title: Execute planned acquisition
items.description: Acquire Bitcoin according to the approved treasury policy. Use limit orders or OTC execution to manage market impact. Ensure all approvals are documented.

items.title: Transfer to institutional custody
items.description: Move all acquired Bitcoin to the institutional custodian. Verify receipt and obtain custody confirmation.

items.title: Lodge ASX announcement for material acquisition
items.description: If the acquisition is material, lodge the prepared ASX announcement. Provide the Company Secretary with full transaction details for the announcement.

categories.name: Ongoing Maintenance Setup
categories.description: Establish processes for ongoing management, reporting, and compliance.

items.title: Establish board reporting cadence
items.description: Set up quarterly (minimum) board reporting covering: Bitcoin holdings, unrealised gains/losses, policy compliance, market developments, and regulatory updates.

items.title: Integrate into half-yearly and annual reporting
items.description: Update financial reporting templates to include Bitcoin note disclosures, management commentary, and risk disclosures for half-yearly and annual reports.

items.title: Prepare AGM materials
items.description: Include Bitcoin treasury information in the annual report and prepare for shareholder questions at the AGM. Brief the chair and CEO on talking points.

items.title: Schedule annual policy review
items.description: Set an annual review date for the Bitcoin Treasury Policy. The review should consider: allocation performance, regulatory changes, shareholder feedback, and market developments.

items.title: Monitor regulatory developments
items.description: Subscribe to ASIC, ASX, and ATO updates relevant to digital assets. Brief the board on any material regulatory changes.
